_meta.source: Based on the New York State Driver's Manual (MV-21), common knowledge points. Last reviewed: 2026-04-17.
_meta.disclaimer: Non-official. For study use only. Always refer to the official NY DMV Driver's Manual for authoritative content.

questions.question: What is the standard speed limit in a school zone in New York State when children are present?
questions.explanation: In New York, school speed zones are posted at 15–20 mph. The most commonly posted limit when children are present is 20 mph. Always obey posted signs.

questions.question: What does a solid yellow center line on your side of the road mean?
options: You may pass when traffic is clear
Passing is prohibited on your side
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means you must not cross it to pass. A broken yellow line means passing is permitted when safe.

questions.question: When must you use your headlights in New York State?
options: From sunset to sunrise and any time visibility is less than 1,000 feet
Only when the sun has fully set
questions.explanation: NY law requires headlights from 30 minutes after sunset to 30 minutes before sunrise, and whenever visibility is less than 1,000 feet due to weather or other conditions.

questions.question: At an intersection with no signs or signals, who has the right-of-way?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach an uncontrolled intersection at the same time, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right. But the vehicle that arrived first generally has the right-of-way.

questions.question: Two vehicles arrive at an uncontrolled intersection at exactly the same time. Which vehicle must yield?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles arrive at an uncontrolled intersection simultaneously, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.question: What does a flashing red traffic light mean?
options: Slow down and proceed with caution
The signal is about to turn green
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated the same as a stop sign: come to a complete stop, yield to traffic and pedestrians, then proceed when safe.

questions.question: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
options: Slow down and proceed with caution
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light is a warning signal. You should slow down, be alert, and proceed carefully.

questions.question: What is the minimum safe following distance (in seconds) under normal conditions?
questions.explanation: The NY DMV recommends at least a 3-second following distance under normal conditions. Increase the gap in bad weather, heavy traffic, or at night.
questions.source: NY DMV Manual – Safe Following Distance

questions.question: When approaching a school bus with flashing red lights and a stop arm extended, you must:
options: Slow to 15 mph and pass carefully
Stop and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing and the arm is withdrawn
Stop only if children are visible
Yield and pass if on the opposite side of a divided highway
questions.explanation: You must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights and extended stop arm on any undivided road. On a divided highway (with a physical barrier), vehicles on the opposite side may proceed, but all others must stop.

questions.question: What is the blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit for drivers 21 and older in New York State?
questions.explanation: In New York, a BAC of 0.08% or higher is considered legally intoxicated (DWI) for drivers 21 and older. For drivers under 21, the limit is 0.02%.

questions.question: What is the BAC limit for drivers under 21 in New York State?
questions.explanation: New York has a Zero Tolerance law. Drivers under 21 with a BAC of 0.02% or higher can face license suspension.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond-shaped sign indicate?
options: A warning about road conditions or hazards ahead
questions.explanation: Diamond-shaped yellow signs are warning signs. They alert you to potential hazards, changes in road conditions, or other situations requiring caution.

questions.question: An octagonal (8-sided) red sign always means:
questions.explanation: The red octagonal shape is used exclusively for STOP signs. When you see one, you must come to a complete stop.

questions.question: A triangular red and white sign means:
questions.explanation: The downward-pointing red triangle is a YIELD sign. You must slow down and give the right-of-way to traffic already in the intersection or about to enter.

questions.question: What does a white rectangular sign with black lettering typically indicate?
options: A regulation that must be obeyed
questions.explanation: White rectangular signs with black (or red) text are regulatory signs. They display laws or regulations you must follow, such as speed limits or turn restrictions.

questions.question: When you hear the siren of an emergency vehicle approaching, you must:
options: Speed up to get out of the way quickly
Pull to the right edge of the road and stop
Slow down and continue at reduced speed
Pull to the left and stop
questions.explanation: New York law requires you to pull to the right side of the road, clear of any intersection, and stop until the emergency vehicle has passed.

questions.question: The 'Move Over' law in New York requires drivers to:
options: Speed up past stopped emergency vehicles
Change to a non-adjacent lane or slow down when passing stopped emergency or hazard vehicles with lights flashing
Stop completely behind any vehicle with flashing lights
questions.explanation: NY's Move Over Law requires drivers to move over one lane (if safe) or slow down significantly when passing stopped emergency, law enforcement, tow trucks, or highway maintenance vehicles displaying flashing lights.
questions.source: NY DMV Manual – Move Over Law

questions.question: When turning left at a green light, you must yield to:
options: Oncoming traffic and pedestrians in the crosswalk
questions.explanation: When making a left turn on a green light, you must yield to all oncoming traffic proceeding through the intersection and to pedestrians in the crosswalk.

questions.question: When may you pass on the right?
options: Never — passing on the right is always illegal
Only when the vehicle ahead is turning left and there is a clear lane to the right
On any two-lane road when the left lane is occupied
questions.explanation: You may pass on the right only when the vehicle ahead is turning left and there is a proper lane or sufficient paved road to the right to do so safely.

questions.question: On a highway, the minimum speed limit is often posted. Driving significantly below the flow of traffic is:
options: A violation that can be as dangerous as speeding
Legal as long as hazard lights are on
Only a problem in the left lane
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly can be as dangerous as speeding. It can cause rear-end collisions and impede the flow of traffic. Obey minimum speed limits and match the reasonable flow of traffic.

questions.question: What is the speed limit in a residential area in New York when no speed limit is posted?
questions.explanation: In New York State, the default speed limit in a city, town, or village (residential area) when no limit is posted is 30 mph.

questions.question: You are driving on an undivided highway and a school bus ahead is loading children. What must you do?
options: Slow to 20 mph and pass carefully
Stop at least 20 feet away from the bus
Pass on the left side only
questions.explanation: On an undivided road, all traffic in both directions must stop at least 20 feet from a school bus that has its red lights flashing and stop arm extended.

questions.question: What does a green arrow traffic signal mean?
options: Proceed with caution in all directions
You may proceed in the direction of the arrow; opposing traffic is stopped
Yield to all pedestrians, then proceed
questions.explanation: A green arrow is a protected signal. Traffic in the direction of the arrow may proceed; conflicting traffic is controlled by a red light.

questions.question: A yellow traffic light means:
options: Speed up before the light turns red
Stop if it is safe to do so
questions.explanation: A steady yellow (amber) light warns that the signal is about to change to red. You should stop if you can do so safely. If you cannot stop safely, proceed with caution.

questions.question: What should you do when you see a pedestrian with a white cane or guide dog at a crosswalk?
options: Honk to let them know you are there
Slow down but do not stop unless they step off the curb
Proceed if the light is green
questions.explanation: Pedestrians who are blind or visually impaired have the absolute right-of-way. You must stop and yield regardless of traffic signal status.

questions.question: At a crosswalk with no traffic signal, you must yield to a pedestrian when:
options: Only if the pedestrian is in your lane
When the pedestrian is in or entering the crosswalk in your lane or adjacent lane
Only if a 'Yield to Pedestrians' sign is posted
questions.explanation: NY law requires drivers to yield to pedestrians in or about to enter a crosswalk. This includes the lane you're in and the adjacent lane.

questions.question: When can you make a right turn on a red light in New York State?
options: After coming to a complete stop and yielding to traffic and pedestrians, unless a sign prohibits it
Without stopping if no traffic is present
questions.explanation: Right turns on red are permitted after a complete stop and after yielding to all traffic and pedestrians, unless a 'No Turn on Red' sign is posted. In New York City, right-on-red is prohibited unless a sign specifically permits it.

questions.question: When parallel parking, your vehicle's wheels must be within how many inches of the curb?
questions.explanation: When parallel parking in New York, you must park within 12 inches (one foot) of the curb.

questions.question: You may NOT park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
questions.explanation: New York law prohibits parking within 15 feet of a fire hydrant at any time.

questions.question: You are approaching a railroad crossing with flashing lights and/or a lowered gate. You must:
options: Slow to 15 mph and proceed carefully
Stop and wait until the lights stop flashing and the gate fully rises
Stop and go as soon as the train passes, even if the gate is still down
Only stop if the train is visible
questions.explanation: At a railroad crossing with activated signals or lowered gates, you must stop and not proceed until the lights stop flashing and the gate is fully raised.

questions.question: What is the penalty for a first-time DWI conviction in New York?
options: Up to 1 year in jail and/or a fine of $500–$1,000, plus license revocation for at least 6 months
questions.explanation: A first DWI (BAC ≥ 0.08%) in NY carries up to 1 year in jail and/or a fine of $500–$1,000, plus at least a 6-month license revocation.

questions.question: What does New York's 'Implied Consent' law mean?
options: You automatically consent to a search of your vehicle
By driving in NY, you consent to a chemical test for alcohol or drugs if lawfully requested by a police officer
You consent to roadside sobriety tests only
You consent to breathalyzer only if you've been drinking
questions.explanation: Under NY's Implied Consent law, any person who operates a motor vehicle in NY is deemed to have given consent to a chemical test (blood, breath, urine, or saliva) to determine BAC or drug content if arrested.

questions.question: Refusing a chemical test (breathalyzer) in New York will result in:
options: No additional penalties beyond the DWI charge
Immediate license revocation for at least 1 year and a civil penalty
Automatic acquittal of the DWI charge
questions.explanation: Refusing a chemical test in NY results in immediate license revocation for at least 1 year (longer for repeat offenses) and a civil penalty, regardless of whether you are guilty of DWI.

questions.question: When must you signal before changing lanes or turning?
options: At least 100 feet (about 30 m) before the turn or lane change
At least 200 feet before on highways and 100 feet on city streets
Only when other vehicles are present
questions.explanation: NY law requires you to signal at least 100 feet before turning in a city, town, or village, and at least 200 feet on highways or roads where the speed limit exceeds 45 mph.

questions.question: What does a 'Do Not Enter' sign look like and mean?
options: A diamond with an X — road closed ahead
A red circle with a white horizontal bar — do not enter this road or lane
A red octagon — stop and then enter
A white square with a red border — no parking
questions.explanation: A 'Do Not Enter' sign is a red circle with a white horizontal bar. It means you must not enter the road or lane, usually because traffic flows the other way.

questions.question: What does a 'Wrong Way' sign indicate?
options: You have entered a one-way street against traffic — leave the road immediately
The lane is closed for construction
You are about to enter a toll road
questions.explanation: A red and white 'Wrong Way' sign means you have entered a roadway going in the wrong direction. You should back up or turn around safely immediately.

questions.question: In a work/construction zone, traffic fines are generally:
options: Reduced because workers are causing delays
The same as in normal zones
questions.explanation: In New York, fines for moving violations committed in designated work zones are doubled.

questions.question: What is the proper hand signal for a left turn?
options: Left arm extended straight out horizontally
questions.explanation: For a left turn, extend your left arm straight out horizontally through the window.

questions.question: What is the proper hand signal for a right turn?
options: Left arm extended straight out horizontally
Left arm bent upward at 90° (pointing up)
Left arm bent downward at 45° (pointing down)
questions.explanation: For a right turn, extend your left arm and bend it upward at a 90° angle (hand pointing up).

questions.question: What is the proper hand signal for slowing down or stopping?
options: Left arm extended straight out horizontally
Left arm extended downward at 45° (pointing down and back)
questions.explanation: For stopping or slowing, extend your left arm downward at 45° with your palm facing backward.

questions.question: When are you required to use your windshield wipers in New York?
options: Whenever precipitation makes it difficult to see clearly, and whenever you use your defroster in precipitation
Only when it's raining hard enough to need them
questions.explanation: NY requires wipers whenever rain, snow, or other precipitation makes it necessary to clear the windshield. NY also requires headlights when wipers are in use.
questions.source: NY DMV Manual – Driving in Bad Weather

questions.question: You must turn on your headlights when using your windshield wipers due to rain, snow, or fog in New York. This rule is:
questions.explanation: NY law requires headlights to be on whenever windshield wipers are in use due to precipitation, reducing visibility, or adverse weather conditions.

questions.question: If your vehicle begins to skid on a slippery road, you should:
options: Ease off the gas, do not brake hard, and steer in the direction you want to go
Turn sharply in the opposite direction of the skid
questions.explanation: During a skid: ease off the accelerator, avoid hard braking (unless ABS), and steer gently in the direction you want the front of the vehicle to go (steer into the skid) to regain control.

questions.question: A solid white line between lanes of traffic going in the same direction means:
options: You must stay in your lane — crossing is prohibited
You may change lanes but should do so with care
The left lane is for passing only
It marks the edge of the road
questions.explanation: A solid white line discourages lane changes; it means you should stay in your lane but are not absolutely prohibited from changing. A broken white line means lane changes are more freely permitted.

questions.question: What does a 'No Passing Zone' sign (pennant shape) indicate?
options: The beginning of a no-passing zone — passing is prohibited ahead
questions.explanation: The yellow pennant (horizontal triangle) shaped sign marks the beginning of a no-passing zone, typically placed on the left side of the road.

questions.question: When driving on a multi-lane road, slower traffic should:
options: Drive in any lane they choose
Keep to the right and use the left lane only for passing
questions.explanation: Slower traffic should keep to the right. Left lanes are intended for passing and faster traffic. Staying in the left lane when not passing can impede traffic flow.

questions.question: If you miss your exit on a highway, you should:
options: Back up on the shoulder to reach your exit
Make a U-turn on the highway
Continue to the next exit and turn around
Stop and wait for a break in traffic
questions.explanation: Never back up or make a U-turn on a highway. Continue to the next exit, leave the highway, and find a safe route back.

questions.question: What should you do if a tire blows out while driving?
options: Brake hard and steer to the side immediately
Hold the steering wheel firmly, ease off the gas gradually, and steer to the right to slow down safely
Accelerate briefly to stabilize the vehicle
questions.explanation: In a blowout: grip the steering wheel firmly, ease off the gas (do NOT brake suddenly), and let the vehicle slow gradually. Then steer gently to the right shoulder.

questions.question: You are involved in a collision that causes injury or property damage over $1,000. What must you do?
options: Report it only if someone is injured
File a written report (MV-104) with the DMV within 10 days
Report it to police within 48 hours only
No report is needed if you exchange information at the scene
questions.explanation: If a collision results in injury, death, or property damage exceeding $1,000, you must file an MV-104 report with the DMV within 10 days.

questions.question: When a vehicle breaks down on the highway, the driver should:
options: Stay in the travel lane and turn on hazard lights
Pull off to the right shoulder, turn on hazard lights, and exit away from traffic
Stay in the vehicle with no lights on
Stand in the roadway to flag down help
questions.explanation: Move to the right shoulder safely, turn on hazard/emergency flashers, and exit on the right (passenger) side away from traffic. Place warning triangles or flares if available.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct statement about motorcycle blind spots?
options: A motorcycle's small size can make it invisible in a car's blind spot; always check mirrors and turn your head
Motorcycles always use the center of the lane
Motorcycles have larger blind spots than cars
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are smaller and can be easily hidden in a car's blind spot. Always check mirrors AND turn your head to scan before changing lanes or turning.

questions.question: When sharing the road with a large truck, you should avoid:
options: Driving in a truck's blind spots (No-Zones) — directly behind, beside, and in front too close
questions.explanation: Large trucks have large 'No-Zone' blind spots to the rear, sides, and very close in front. Avoid lingering in these areas; if you can't see the truck driver in their mirror, they can't see you.

questions.question: Bicyclists on the road have:
options: No legal rights — cyclists must always yield to cars
The same rights and responsibilities as motor vehicle drivers
The right-of-way only on dedicated bike lanes
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the same rights and responsibilities as motor vehicle operators. Drivers must share the road and give cyclists a safe passing distance (at least 3 feet in NY).

questions.question: What is the minimum passing distance you must give a bicyclist when overtaking them?
questions.explanation: New York law requires motor vehicle operators to give cyclists at least 3 feet of clearance when passing.

questions.question: When approaching a yield sign, you should:
options: Slow down, check for cross traffic, and stop only if necessary to yield the right-of-way
Proceed normally; yield signs are advisory only
questions.explanation: At a yield sign, slow down and be prepared to stop to give the right-of-way to traffic already in the intersection or road. Stop only if necessary.

questions.question: Distracted driving includes which of the following?
options: Eating, texting, and adjusting the radio while driving
questions.explanation: Distracted driving includes any activity that takes your attention from driving: texting, eating, drinking, using a phone, adjusting radio or GPS, talking with passengers, grooming, etc.

questions.question: In New York, using a handheld mobile phone while driving is:
options: Legal if you're stopped at a red light
Illegal; you must use a hands-free device
Legal as long as you use speaker mode
questions.explanation: NY law prohibits handheld mobile phone use while driving. You must use a hands-free device. This applies even while stopped at a red light.

questions.question: Texting while driving in New York carries a fine and how many points on your license?
questions.explanation: Using a handheld electronic device (including texting) while driving results in a fine and 5 points added to your driving record in New York.

questions.question: What should you do when you see a 'Slow Moving Vehicle' emblem (orange triangle) on the road ahead?
options: Slow down and prepare to pass safely when it is legal to do so
Honk to signal the vehicle to move over
questions.explanation: An orange reflective triangle on the back of a vehicle (e.g., farm equipment) indicates it moves at 25 mph or less. Slow down and pass when it is safe and legal to do so.

questions.question: What color are warning signs related to construction and work zones?
questions.explanation: Work zone/construction warning signs in the United States are orange with black lettering or symbols.

questions.question: When entering a highway from an on-ramp, you should:
options: Stop at the end of the ramp and wait for a gap
Use the acceleration lane to match the speed of highway traffic, then merge smoothly
Yield to all highway traffic by slowing on the ramp
Merge immediately at the bottom of the ramp regardless of speed
questions.explanation: Use the acceleration (on-ramp) lane to reach highway speed, then merge when there is a safe gap. Yield to traffic already on the highway.

questions.question: What does a double solid yellow center line mean?
options: Passing is permitted in both directions
Passing is prohibited in both directions
questions.explanation: A double solid yellow center line means no passing is allowed in either direction. Vehicles on both sides must stay in their lane.

questions.question: When driving in fog, you should use:
options: No lights — they reduce visibility further
questions.explanation: In fog, use low-beam headlights or fog lights. High beams reflect off the fog and reduce visibility. Also reduce your speed significantly.
questions.source: NY DMV Manual – Driving in Bad Weather

questions.question: Hydroplaning occurs when:
options: Your wipers fail in heavy rain
A layer of water builds between your tires and the road, causing loss of traction
Your engine overheats from driving through standing water
Your vehicle is pushed by crosswinds
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning happens when water builds up under the tires faster than it can be displaced, causing the tires to ride on a film of water and losing contact with the road surface.
questions.source: NY DMV Manual – Driving in Bad Weather

questions.question: To avoid hydroplaning, you should:
options: Drive faster to skim over water
Reduce speed in wet conditions and keep tires properly inflated
questions.explanation: Reduce speed in wet conditions, keep tires properly inflated and with adequate tread depth. Avoid cruise control in rain and avoid large puddles when possible.
questions.source: NY DMV Manual – Driving in Bad Weather

questions.question: What is the maximum number of points you can accumulate on your NY license in 18 months before your license is suspended?
questions.explanation: Accumulating 11 or more points within any 18-month period will result in a license suspension in New York.

questions.question: How many points does speeding 1–10 mph over the limit add to a NY driver's license?
questions.explanation: Speeding 1–10 mph over the posted limit adds 3 points to your NY driving record. Speeding 11–20 mph over adds 4 points, and higher violations add more.

questions.question: You must stop and yield to pedestrians at a crosswalk even if it is NOT marked, in New York.
options: False — only marked crosswalks require stopping
True — both marked and unmarked crosswalks at intersections require yielding to pedestrians
False — pedestrians must wait for the walk signal
questions.explanation: In NY, crosswalks exist at every intersection, whether painted or not. You must yield to pedestrians at all crosswalks.

questions.question: When you are about to be passed by another vehicle, you should:
options: Speed up to maintain your gap
Keep your speed and stay in your lane — do not accelerate
Move to the right lane immediately
Slow down and let them pass by pulling over
questions.explanation: When being passed, maintain your speed and position. Do not accelerate. You may move slightly to the right within your lane to give extra space.

questions.question: In New York, all front-seat passengers must wear a seat belt. Rear-seat passengers are:
options: Not required to wear seat belts if over 16
Required to wear seat belts at all ages
Only required to belt up on highways
Only required to belt up if under 18
questions.explanation: All occupants of a motor vehicle in New York — front and rear — are required to wear seat belts at all times, regardless of age.

questions.question: Children under what age must be secured in an appropriate child safety seat in New York?
options: There is no child seat law
questions.explanation: In NY, children under 8 years old must be secured in an appropriate child restraint system (car seat or booster seat). Children under 4 must be in a federally approved child safety seat.

questions.question: What does 'right-of-way' mean?
options: The legal right to always proceed without stopping
The privilege of immediate use of the roadway, which must be yielded when required by law
The right to park on any road
The right to drive in any lane
questions.explanation: Right-of-way is the privilege of having immediate use of a portion of a roadway. No driver is ever granted an absolute right-of-way — it must be yielded in certain situations.

questions.question: When should you use your horn?
options: To express frustration at other drivers
Only when necessary to avoid a collision or warn others of danger
questions.explanation: The horn should be used only to warn others and avoid danger, not to express anger or greet friends. Unnecessary horn use can be illegal in some areas and contributes to road rage.
questions.source: NY DMV Manual – Sharing the Road

questions.question: You are driving at night. An approaching vehicle has its high beams on. You should:
options: Flash your high beams repeatedly to signal the other driver
Turn on your own high beams
Look toward the right edge of the road ahead and avoid staring at the lights
questions.explanation: Do not stare at oncoming headlights. Look toward the right edge of the road to help avoid being blinded. Do not flash your own high beams.

questions.question: High-beam headlights must be dimmed within how many feet of an oncoming vehicle?
questions.explanation: In New York, you must dim your high beams when you are within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle, and within 200 feet when following another vehicle.

questions.question: When following another vehicle at night, you must switch to low beams within how many feet?
questions.explanation: When following another vehicle, switch to low beams within 200 feet to avoid blinding the driver ahead through their mirrors.

questions.question: A pennant-shaped sign is always placed:
options: On the right side of the road
On the left side of the road
Above the road on a gantry
questions.explanation: The no-passing zone pennant sign is always placed on the LEFT side of the road facing the driver to warn that passing is prohibited ahead.

questions.question: What should you do before backing up your vehicle?
options: Turn on hazard lights and proceed
Turn your body and look over your right shoulder; also check all mirrors for obstacles
questions.explanation: Before backing up, turn your body and look over your right shoulder out the rear window. Also check all mirrors and be aware of pedestrians, cyclists, and small objects behind you.

questions.question: Orange-colored road signs are used to indicate:
questions.explanation: Orange signs are used exclusively for construction and work zones. They indicate temporary conditions and changed traffic patterns.

questions.question: Blue road signs indicate:
options: Services such as gas, food, lodging, and hospitals
questions.explanation: Blue signs provide information about motorist services, such as gas stations, food, lodging, hospitals, and tourist information centers.
